children.text: Report on a university drama project
Drama student Imogen Clare-Wood describes a collaborative theatre project she took part in

This project was born from discussions I had with other drama students about the kind of theatre productions we'd like to create. Although we had previously been involved in various university productions where we took on specific roles, such as costume designer, producer, director and so on, we now wanted to take a collaborative approach that was new to all of us. We wanted a project in which every voice would carry equal weight and everyone would be able to contribute to every aspect of the show. We decided that the way to achieve this feeling of collaboration would be to concentrate on the process by which the work emerged during auditions and rehearsals. We chose a play that we felt allowed varied interpretations – Harold Pinter's The Lover, a modern one-act play with just two characters. We decided we would use six actors cast as three different couples, and each pair would have a turn to perform on one of the three nights that the play was to be staged.

The next step was to hold auditions in order to find six actors who felt the same way about the project as we did. We were anxious not to create an “us-and-them” feeling to the auditions, since collective ownership of the project was crucial to our ideas. So we ran the first round of auditions as a series of workshops, which we kept very informal and relaxed. Then we recalled fifteen actors for the second round of auditions. At this stage we wanted the reactions and ideas to flow and be explored by the group in the form of a discussion. We were looking for actors who could encourage other people's ideas but who were also eager to input their own thoughts. Possibly the hardest part of the whole process was sitting down afterwards and deciding on the final six actors who would take part in the project.

Our initial rehearsals were dedicated to creating a strong group dynamic and to exploring some of the themes of the play without explicit reference to the script. One of the strongest tools we used for this was freewriting – an exercise in which one person would read out a list of unconnected words and the rest of the group would clear their minds and simply write something in response to those words. We found it helped us to gain insight into the different ways our minds work. It also meant that we got to know each other very well, very quickly!

It was only during the next phase of rehearsals that we finally began to work with the script. We had not yet decided on the pairings for the six actors at this point, and we had the non-actors on the team reading in addition to the people who would be doing the final performances. The whole team finally decided on the pairings of the actors by a vote, and, miraculously, it was a unanimous decision. The casting of the roles worked well and felt more natural than it had in any other show I've worked on. I think the dynamic that we developed over the early rehearsals was crucial in giving us three such strong onstage relationships.

Other decisions were made in much the same way. The publicity was the responsibility of one member of the team, but she asked everyone what they thought would be effective. She then made several different versions, and from these we chose the design we liked the most. The stage set was left more to the individual pairs of actors, but since a large part of the later rehearsals was observing and feeding back on the individual performances being created by each pair, everyone was able to help with set design. We initially planned that each pair would use the same stage set. However, due to the different ways they interpreted the script, it became important for the set to be adapted for each couple.

In the final performances, it was surprising that despite the collaborative nature of the project, a range of very different interpretations emerged. This is, of course, partly due to the tragicomic nature of Pinter's text, which allowed the pairs of actors to exploit these two disparate elements, tragedy and comedy, to different degrees.

The question-and-answer session held after the show was perhaps the most rewarding part of the project. The questions that the audience asked were interesting and insightful, and certainly made everyone in the team think about what we'd been doing, what we'd wanted to achieve and whether we'd achieved it. We were asked, among other things, about how successful our collaborative ethic had been, how we'd ended up with three such different shows, how we'd overcome the initial "production-team-versus-cast” divide, and how we would continue in the future.
from the passage for each answer.
Started with the wish for a collaborative project focusing on the
first round: organised as several workshops – fifteen actors were then selected
– the final six actors were then selected
was found to be an effective technique
was only used in later rehearsals
to decide which actor would play each part
everyone was shown different designs for the publicity and the best one was chosen
this resulted in some interesting comments

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：项目起源 —— 源于对协作项目的愿景，该项目聚焦于戏剧制作的_\___\__
答案：process（过程）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “We decided that the way to achieve this feeling of collaboration would be to concentrate on the process by which the work emerged during auditions and rehearsals”，明确提到项目核心是 “专注于戏剧制作的过程（process）”，包括试镜和排练阶段，与题干 “focusing on the... of producing a play” 对应，因此答案为 “process”。
题目翻译：试镜 —— 第二轮：以_\___\__形式组织 —— 随后选出最终 6 名演员
答案：discussion（讨论）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “Then we recalled fifteen actors for the second round of auditions. At this stage we wanted the reactions and ideas to flow and be explored by the group in the form of a discussion”，明确指出第二轮试镜以 “小组讨论（discussion）” 形式开展，与题干 “organised as a...” 对应，因此答案为 “discussion”。
题目翻译：准备阶段 —— 在早期排练中，\___\___被发现是一种有效的技巧
答案：freewriting（自由写作）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “One of the strongest tools we used for this was freewriting – an exercise in which one person would read out a list of unconnected words and the rest of the group would clear their minds and simply write something in response to those words”，“strongest tools” 对应题干 “effective technique”，明确提到 “自由写作（freewriting）” 是早期排练的有效技巧，因此答案为 “freewriting”。
题目翻译：准备阶段 ——\___\___仅在后期排练中使用
答案：script（剧本）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “It was only during the next phase of rehearsals that we finally began to work with the script”，明确指出 “剧本（script）” 在排练的下一阶段（即后期）才开始使用，与题干 “only used in later rehearsals” 对应，因此答案为 “script”。
题目翻译：准备阶段 —— 所有人通过_\___\__决定每位演员的角色分配
答案：vote（投票）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “The whole team finally decided on the pairings of the actors by a vote, and, miraculously, it was a unanimous decision”，“decided on the pairings” 对应题干 “decide which actor would play each part”，明确提到通过 “投票（vote）” 确定演员配对，因此答案为 “vote”。
题目翻译：演出阶段 ——\___\___的多样性令人惊讶
答案：interpretations（解读）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “In the final performances, it was surprising that despite the collaborative nature of the project, a range of very different interpretations emerged”，“a range of very different” 对应题干 “the variety of”，明确提到演出呈现出 “多种不同的解读（interpretations）”，因此答案为 “interpretations”。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：Origin of project - Started with the wish for a collaborative project focusing on the \___\___ of producing a play
题目翻译：项目起源 —— 源于对协作项目的愿景，该项目聚焦于戏剧制作的_\___\__
答案：process（过程）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “We decided that the way to achieve this feeling of collaboration would be to concentrate on the process by which the work emerged during auditions and rehearsals”，明确提到项目核心是 “专注于戏剧制作的过程（process）”，包括试镜和排练阶段，与题干 “focusing on the... of producing a play” 对应，因此答案为 “process”。

（2）题目 2：Auditions - second round: organised as a \___\___ – the final six actors were then selected
题目翻译：试镜 —— 第二轮：以_\___\__形式组织 —— 随后选出最终 6 名演员
答案：discussion（讨论）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “Then we recalled fifteen actors for the second round of auditions. At this stage we wanted the reactions and ideas to flow and be explored by the group in the form of a discussion”，明确指出第二轮试镜以 “小组讨论（discussion）” 形式开展，与题干 “organised as a...” 对应，因此答案为 “discussion”。

（3）题目 3：Preparation - in early rehearsals, \___\___ was found to be an effective technique
题目翻译：准备阶段 —— 在早期排练中，\___\___被发现是一种有效的技巧
答案：freewriting（自由写作）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “One of the strongest tools we used for this was freewriting – an exercise in which one person would read out a list of unconnected words and the rest of the group would clear their minds and simply write something in response to those words”，“strongest tools” 对应题干 “effective technique”，明确提到 “自由写作（freewriting）” 是早期排练的有效技巧，因此答案为 “freewriting”。

（4）题目 4：Preparation - the \___\___ was only used in later rehearsals
题目翻译：准备阶段 ——\___\___仅在后期排练中使用
答案：script（剧本）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “It was only during the next phase of rehearsals that we finally began to work with the script”，明确指出 “剧本（script）” 在排练的下一阶段（即后期）才开始使用，与题干 “only used in later rehearsals” 对应，因此答案为 “script”。

（5）题目 5：Preparation - everyone had a \___\___ to decide which actor would play each part
题目翻译：准备阶段 —— 所有人通过_\___\__决定每位演员的角色分配
答案：vote（投票）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “The whole team finally decided on the pairings of the actors by a vote, and, miraculously, it was a unanimous decision”，“decided on the pairings” 对应题干 “decide which actor would play each part”，明确提到通过 “投票（vote）” 确定演员配对，因此答案为 “vote”。

（6）题目 6：Performance - the variety of \___\___ was surprising
题目翻译：演出阶段 ——\___\___的多样性令人惊讶
答案：interpretations（解读）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “In the final performances, it was surprising that despite the collaborative nature of the project, a range of very different interpretations emerged”，“a range of very different” 对应题干 “the variety of”，明确提到演出呈现出 “多种不同的解读（interpretations）”，因此答案为 “interpretations”。

children.text: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 7–13 on your answer sheet, write:
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information about this
The writer had been involved in other collaborative theatre productions at university.
Each actor chosen for the project would play one role for three nights.
The early rehearsals took place at the university theatre.
The writer felt satisfied that all the actors were paired with the right partner.
Some team members discovered an unexpected talent for set design.
The original intention was to use a different stage set for each performance.
The question-and-answer session encouraged the team to think about their aims.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：作者此前曾参与过大学的其他协作式戏剧制作。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “although we had previously been involved in various university productions where we took on specific roles... we now wanted to take a collaborative approach that was new to all of us”，明确提到 “协作模式对所有人都是全新的”，即作者此前未参与过协作式戏剧制作，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：项目中选出的每位演员会在三个晚上都扮演同一个角色。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “six actors cast as three different couples, and each pair would have a turn to perform on one of the three nights that the play was to be staged”，说明 6 名演员分为 3 组，每组在三个晚上中轮流表演一晚，即每位演员仅表演一晚，而非 “三个晚上都扮演同一个角色”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：早期排练在大学剧院进行。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：文章 Paragraph 3 仅详细描述了早期排练的内容（如自由写作）和目的（建立团队氛围），但未提及排练地点 “是否为大学剧院”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：作者对所有演员都配对到合适的搭档感到满意。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “The casting of the roles worked well and felt more natural than it had in any other show I’ve worked on”，“worked well”“more natural” 体现作者对演员配对的认可与满意，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：部分团队成员发现自己在布景设计方面有意外的天赋。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 5 提到 “everyone was able to help with set design”（所有人都能参与布景设计），但未提及 “是否有人发现自己有布景设计天赋”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：最初的计划是为每场演出使用不同的布景。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “We initially planned that each pair would use the same stage set. However, due to the different ways they interpreted the script, it became important for the set to be adapted for each couple”，明确提到最初计划 “三组演员使用相同布景”，后期因解读不同才调整为不同布景，与题干 “最初计划使用不同布景” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：问答环节促使团队思考他们的目标。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “The questions that the audience asked were interesting and insightful, and certainly made everyone in the team think about what we’d been doing, what we’d wanted to achieve and whether we’d achieved it”，“what we’d wanted to achieve” 对应题干 “their aims”，明确提到问答环节促使团队思考目标，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

items.text:
（1）题目 7：The writer had been involved in other collaborative theatre productions at university.
题目翻译：作者此前曾参与过大学的其他协作式戏剧制作。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “although we had previously been involved in various university productions where we took on specific roles... we now wanted to take a collaborative approach that was new to all of us”，明确提到 “协作模式对所有人都是全新的”，即作者此前未参与过协作式戏剧制作，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（2）题目 8：Each actor chosen for the project would play one role for three nights.
题目翻译：项目中选出的每位演员会在三个晚上都扮演同一个角色。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “six actors cast as three different couples, and each pair would have a turn to perform on one of the three nights that the play was to be staged”，说明 6 名演员分为 3 组，每组在三个晚上中轮流表演一晚，即每位演员仅表演一晚，而非 “三个晚上都扮演同一个角色”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（3）题目 9：The early rehearsals took place at the university theatre.
题目翻译：早期排练在大学剧院进行。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：文章 Paragraph 3 仅详细描述了早期排练的内容（如自由写作）和目的（建立团队氛围），但未提及排练地点 “是否为大学剧院”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（4）题目 10：The writer felt satisfied that all the actors were paired with the right partner.
题目翻译：作者对所有演员都配对到合适的搭档感到满意。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “The casting of the roles worked well and felt more natural than it had in any other show I’ve worked on”，“worked well”“more natural” 体现作者对演员配对的认可与满意，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（5）题目 11：Some team members discovered an unexpected talent for set design.
题目翻译：部分团队成员发现自己在布景设计方面有意外的天赋。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 5 提到 “everyone was able to help with set design”（所有人都能参与布景设计），但未提及 “是否有人发现自己有布景设计天赋”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（6）题目 12：The original intention was to use a different stage set for each performance.
题目翻译：最初的计划是为每场演出使用不同的布景。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “We initially planned that each pair would use the same stage set. However, due to the different ways they interpreted the script, it became important for the set to be adapted for each couple”，明确提到最初计划 “三组演员使用相同布景”，后期因解读不同才调整为不同布景，与题干 “最初计划使用不同布景” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（7）题目 13：The question-and-answer session encouraged the team to think about their aims.
题目翻译：问答环节促使团队思考他们的目标。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “The questions that the audience asked were interesting and insightful, and certainly made everyone in the team think about what we’d been doing, what we’d wanted to achieve and whether we’d achieved it”，“what we’d wanted to achieve” 对应题干 “their aims”，明确提到问答环节促使团队思考目标，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
